sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחֲנֶה, apoc. וַיִּחַן-

(1) to bow down, to incline (to decline). (Kindred roots are חָנַן, עָנָח. Arab. حنا to bend, to incline; metaph. to be inclined to any thing; compare חָפֵץ.) Jud. 19:9 , הִנֵּה חֲנֹות הַיֹּום “behold the inclining of the day,” the day already declining. Hence חֲנִית a spear, from its flexibility.

(2) to set oneself down, to pitch one’s tent, Gen 26:17 to encamp, Exo 13:20 17:1 19:2 . Num 1:50, בַּחֲנֹות הַמִּשְׁכָּן “where the tabernacle is let down,” i.e. is pitched.-(a) followed by עַל to encamp against any person or city; hence to besiege, Psa 27:3 2Sa 12:28 Isa 29:3. Followed by an acc. id. Psa 53:6.-(b) to defend any thing, followed by לְ Zec 9:8 compare Psa 34:8.

(3) to inhabit, Isa 29:1.

Derivatives, חָנוּת, חֲנִית, מַחֲנֶה, תַּחֲנֹות pr.n. תַּחַן.
